assumptions: The intragroup dividend receivable has been recognised as a financial asset or monetary item in the consolidated accounting analysis.
The question concerns hedge accounting for foreign exchange risk in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities and components of such items.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for variability in cash flows of recognised items or highly probable forecast transactions, including certain intragroup foreign currency exposures in consolidation.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements.
